mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance against rate changes (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of elevated inflation signals supply-side bottlenecks that erode corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 200 and INDPRO rises, or the negative predictive power vanishes in high-inflation subsamples (Rank IC < 0).

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising wage income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or predictive accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.5% indicates restrictive monetary policy that sharply curtails liquidity and credit availability, forcing immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 records positive 5-day returns in over 65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from noise.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand despite aggregate growth, prompting profit warnings and negative market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 58% of cases, or the interaction term loses significance in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during low unemployment periods signals structural supply constraints that limit output growth, resulting in negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during low unemployment regimes produce positive 60-day forward returns in over 60% of instances, or the regime-specific signal shows Rank IC below 0.01.
